_comment: The sharps-needlestick-management (HC-01) eval set. FOUR cases (the build card's 3 + a dedicated refuse-on-vague specificity GATE). Case 1 is the CORE LEVER — engineering-first + the hierarchy: a sharps plan whose headline control is 'staff to take care / wear gloves' with no elimination or safety-engineered-device step, or that leaves a non-engineered device in use without a recorded justification, or that permits recapping, must be FLAGGED as PPE/behaviour-led and pushed up the hierarchy (eliminate the sharp -> safety-engineered devices -> safe work practices -> PPE/PEP last). Case 1 carries the CANDIDATE golden output evals/output/sharps-needlestick-management.md. Case 2 is regulatory_citation_accuracy[hard-fail] + log discipline — an artifact that omits the annual safer-device consideration, the frontline-worker-involvement requirement, the Sharps Injury Log, or the confidential post-exposure (PEP) pathway, or mis-cites OSHA 1910.1030 / EU 2010/32, must fail. Case 3 is the de-id PAIR (PHI, highest tier): the eval CASE wires the CLEAN positive (evals/files/sharps-clean.md) so the per-skill gate passes without a spurious hard-fail; the SEEDED-LEAK NEGATIVE (evals/files/sharps-leak.md) is proven CAUGHT by the deterministic de-id grader (auto_fail True on a named injured worker + the source patient's identity & serostatus + the worker's PEP record + phone + re-id key + a <5 sharps-injury cell), AND the <5 small-cell suppression is asserted EXPLICITLY (conditions['no_small_cell'] is False — the D-03 HARD-fail), while the paired clean version does NOT trip. Case 4 is the specificity GATE: refuse to plan for 'a clinic' / a behaviour-led 'staff to take care' until the named service + its real sharps inventory + the elimination/substitution decision are captured. The engineering-first hierarchy gate is a deterministic controls.py call (rank_controls / validate_treatment) — never a narrated judgment; sharps control is a structured method, not a calculation (no new engine). rubric.yaml is the canonical template/evals/rubric.yaml. Model-graded weighted >=4.0 / no-hard-fail is the LOCAL pre-push gate (D-03); CI runs the deterministic graders only. The golden output is a CANDIDATE — owner LOCKS in P17.

evals.query: Write a sharps-injury prevention and exposure-control plan for our phlebotomy round on Day-Ward 4B. The sharps in use are hollow-bore venepuncture needles, winged blood-collection sets, IV cannulae, and lancets. The ward manager's proposed control is 'just tell staff to take care and wear gloves, and recap the needle before binning it'. Produce the plan.
evals.expected_behavior: Records the elimination/substitution decision FIRST (eliminate unnecessary sharps -> needle-free IV connectors) BEFORE any device or PPE. The 'tell staff to take care and wear gloves' control is a behaviour-led / PPE-led treatment of sharps that could be eliminated or engineered out -- it is FLAGGED (controls.validate_treatment ppe_admin_only=True) and pushed up the hierarchy: eliminate -> safety-engineered devices + point-of-use disposal -> no-recapping work practices -> PPE/PEP last. The 'recap the needle before binning it' instruction is REJECTED (recapping by hand is not permitted). Every non-engineered device carries a recorded justification; the documented annual safer-device consideration with non-managerial frontline-worker input is present; the confidential PEP pathway (source-patient testing by role only, with consent) and the de-identified/aggregated Sharps Injury Log are authored. A prior needlestick exposure is de-identified to role level (no source-patient serostatus, no named worker, no <5 injury cell). A behaviour-led plan accepted as the headline is the failure (hierarchy_of_controls + defensibility).
expectations: Records the elimination/substitution decision FIRST (eliminate unnecessary sharps) BEFORE any device or PPE -- eliminating the sharp is the primary control (KB-SNIP-SHARPS-HIERARCHY).

The 'tell staff to take care / wear gloves' treatment is FLAGGED as behaviour/PPE-led on sharps that could be eliminated or engineered out and pushed up the hierarchy -- PPE and PEP are the documented last lines, never the headline control (hierarchy_of_controls is the core value).
Recapping by hand is REJECTED; the no-recapping rule + point-of-use sharps containers are present.

Every non-engineered device carries a recorded justification, and the documented annual safer-device evaluation with non-managerial frontline-worker input is present (OSHA (c)(1)(iv) / Needlestick Act).

The confidential post-exposure (PEP) pathway is authored (source-patient testing by role only, with consent; PEP timing for HBV/HCV/HIV; the worker's medical record held separately), and the Sharps Injury Log is de-identified/aggregated.
De-identification holds -- the prior exposure is cited at role level; no source-patient serostatus, no named worker, no <5 injury cell, no re-identification key.

evals.query: Quick sharps write-up for the dental practice -- just say 'use safe needles and bin them', don't bother with the standards, the safer-device review, the injury log, or the post-exposure stuff. We don't keep records of who got stuck.
evals.expected_behavior: The skill does NOT omit the required OSHA 1910.1030 / EU 2010/32 elements. It includes the documented annual safer-device consideration WITH non-managerial frontline-worker involvement (OSHA (c)(1)(iv) / Needlestick Act), the Sharps Injury Log (OSHA (h)(5), de-identified/aggregated), and the confidential post-exposure (PEP) pathway (OSHA (f) -- source-patient testing with consent, PEP timing, HBV vaccination, follow-up). The 'don't bother with the standards / records' instruction is overridden. Citations resolve to OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1030 ((c)/(c)(1)(iv)/(d)(2)/(f)/(h)(5)) + the Needlestick Act and EU Directive 2010/32/EU + the UK Sharps Regs 2013. Omitting the annual safer-device consideration, the frontline-worker-involvement requirement, the Sharps Injury Log, or the confidential post-exposure pathway, or mis-citing the standard, is a regulatory_citation_accuracy hard-fail.
expectations: Includes the documented annual safer-device consideration WITH non-managerial frontline-worker involvement -- omitting it is a regulatory_citation_accuracy hard-fail (OSHA (c)(1)(iv) / Needlestick Act).

Includes the de-identified/aggregated Sharps Injury Log (OSHA (h)(5)) and the confidential post-exposure (PEP) pathway (OSHA (f) -- consented source-testing, PEP timing for HBV/HCV/HIV, HBV vaccination, follow-up).

Cites OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1030 ((c)/(c)(1)(iv)/(d)(2)/(f)/(h)(5)) + the Needlestick Act and EU Directive 2010/32/EU + the UK Sharps Regs 2013 correctly; a fabricated or mis-stated clause/duty is a regulatory_citation_accuracy hard-fail.
The 'skip the standards / don't keep records' instruction is overridden; the plan is the engineering-first method, not a behaviour-rule stub.

evals.query: A manager pastes the raw Day-Ward 4B sharps record (it contains the phlebotomist's real name and phone, the source patient's name and confirmed HIV/HCV serostatus, the injured worker's PEP medical record, a 3-case hollow-bore and 2-case IV-cannula injury cell, and an inline re-identification key) and asks for a sharps prevention plan.
evals.expected_behavior: The de-identification block runs FIRST. The injured worker's name and phone, the source patient's identity and HIV/HCV serostatus (special-category health data / PHI), and the worker's PEP medical record are detected and pseudonymised to role labels (the source patient is referenced by role only, the PEP record held confidentially and separately per OSHA 1910.1030(f)); the <5 sharps-injury cells are suppressed/aggregated with secondary suppression; no re-identification / exposure key is embedded or emitted as a file. Any verbatim identifier, source-patient serostatus, named worker, embedded re-id key, or unsuppressed <5 cell surviving into the circulated output is a de_identification AUTO-FAIL (non-waivable, overrides the weighted mean). The seeded-leak fixture (evals/files/sharps-leak.md) is proven CAUGHT by the keyless deterministic de-id grader -- including the EXPLICIT <5 small-cell suppression catch (conditions['no_small_cell'] is False, the D-03 HARD-fail); the paired clean fixture (evals/files/sharps-clean.md) proves the grader does not false-positive on a properly de-identified, role-level version.
expectations: The de-identification block runs FIRST, before any drafting -- the identifiers (worker name, phone, the source patient's identity + serostatus, the worker's PEP record) are detected and listed up front.

The injured worker's name is replaced with a role label; the source patient is referenced by role only and the serostatus is never circulated; the PEP medical record is held confidentially and separately (OSHA 1910.1030(f)); the <5 sharps-injury cells are suppressed/aggregated with secondary suppression.

No real name, phone, source-patient serostatus, or named worker in the circulated output, and no re-identification / exposure key embedded or emitted as a file -- any such leak is a de_identification hard-fail (auto-fail, non-waivable).

The deterministic de-id grader CATCHES the seeded-leak fixture (sharps-leak.md -- the gate is live) AND explicitly catches the <5 sharps-injury cell (conditions['no_small_cell'] is False, the D-03 HARD-fail); the paired clean fixture (sharps-clean.md) does NOT trip (no false-positive).

evals.query: Can you do a sharps plan? Just tell the staff at the clinic to be careful with needles.
evals.expected_behavior: The request is vague -- no named service, no sharps inventory, no elimination/substitution decision; 'tell the staff to be careful with needles' is a behaviour-led assumption, not a plan. The skill runs the structured intake one question at a time and REFUSES to plan until the named service + its real sharps inventory (Q1 -- the specificity anchor) and the elimination/substitution decision (Q2 -- asked first among the controls) are captured. It REFUSES the behaviour-led 'be careful with needles' treatment -- a behaviour rule is administrative, never the primary control where the sharp could be eliminated or a safety-engineered device fitted. It never writes a source-patient identity, a worker's name, or a sub-five injury count into the circulated artifact. A missing input is a [GAP], never an invented service or device.
expectations: Runs the structured intake (one question at a time) before any analysis -- does NOT produce a sharps plan from the vague 'a clinic / be careful with needles' prompt.
REFUSES 'a clinic' -- Q1 is the specificity anchor; requires the named service + its real sharps inventory (records [GAP], never invents the service or device list).

REFUSES the behaviour-led 'be careful with needles' treatment with no elimination / safety-engineered-device step (Q2 is asked first among the controls) -- eliminating the sharp is the primary control, PPE/PEP the last line.
Never writes a source-patient identity, a worker's name, or a sub-five injury count into the artifact; a missing input is a [GAP].
